title: Systems and methods of privacy within a security system
abstract: Systems and methods disclosed herein provide setting a mode for a door lock of a door in a building with an electronic device communicatively coupled to the door lock via a communications interface of the door lock, when the mode of the door lock is set in a privacy mode, correspondingly placing a security system device communicatively coupled to the door lock into a privacy mode via the communications interface, and updating an operation state of the security system in the building according to the setting of the privacy mode for the door lock and the device.
claims:
Claims 1. A method comprising: setting a mode for a door lock of a door in a building with an electronic device communicatively coupled to the door lock via a communications interface of the door lock; when the mode of the door lock is set in a privacy mode, correspondingly placing a security system device communicatively coupled to the door lock into a privacy mode via the communications interface; and updating an operation state of the security system in the building according to the setting of the privacy mode for the door lock and the device. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the setting a mode for the door lock comprises: setting the mode for at least one from the group consisting of: a virtual door lock, a room status, a floor status in the building, an area status in the building, a doorbell, and one or more sensors of a room. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the setting the mode of the door lock and the device to the privacy mode comprises at least partially disabling the receipt of notification messages to the electronic device. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the setting the mode of the device to the privacy mode comprises at least partially disabling the device of the security system, wherein the device is selected from the group consisting of: a motion sensor, a thermal sensor, a light sensor, and a video camera. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the setting the mode of the device to the privacy mode comprises refraining from providing data from the device to the security system when the privacy mode is enabled. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein setting the mode of the door lock to the privacy mode prohibits viewing of data captured by the device of the security system via an application on the electronic device. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the setting the mode of the door lock and the device to the privacy mode enables a sensor of the security system for an exterior of the building and at least partially disables a sensor of the security system in an interior of the building. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the setting the mode of the device to the privacy mode disables message notifications from the device to the electronic device. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein the setting of the mode of the door lock to the privacy mode at least partially disables at least one of the group consisting of: an audible output from a doorbell for the building when a button on the doorbell is selected, and an intercom of the building. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the setting of the mode of the door lock to the privacy mode controls the security system to activate a movement of window treatments corresponding to a window in a room having the door lock, so that the movement of the window treatments covers the window when the privacy mode is activated. 11. The method of claim 1, wherein the setting of the mode of the door lock to the privacy mode comprises: selecting, from a user interface of the electronic device, the privacy mode of the security system for at least one of the group consisting of: a room of the building, and the interior of the building. 12. The method of claim 1, wherein the setting of the mode of the door lock to the privacy mode enables the security system to perform at least one from the group consisting of: turning off lighting in a room and reducing the light output by lighting devices within a room by a predetermined level. 13. The method of claim 1, wherein the setting of the mode of the door lock to the privacy mode enables the security system to close a garage door of the building. 14. The method of claim 1, wherein the setting of the mode of the door lock to the privacy mode at least partially disables the electronic device from receiving at least one of the group consisting of: phone calls, e-mail, and text messages. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the setting of the mode of the door lock to the privacy mode configures the electronic device to receive communications from only a preset list of contacts. 16. The method of claim 1, wherein the setting of the mode of the door lock to the privacy mode is disabled when a safety event is detected by the security system. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the detected safety event is from a group consisting of: a detected smoke event, a detected fire event, and a break-in event. 18. The method of claim 16, further comprising: transmitting, with the security system, a notification to the electronic device when the privacy mode is disabled. 19. The method of claim 1, wherein setting the mode of the door lock to the privacy mode comprises at least one from the group consisting of: determining whether a user is within a room associated with the door lock and determining whether the electronic device is within the room associated with the door lock. 20. A system comprising: a door lock of a door in a building, wherein the door lock has a plurality of modes of operation and includes a communications interface; a device of a security system of the building communicatively coupled to the door lock via the communications interface; and an electronic device communicatively coupled to the door lock via the communications interface to set the mode of the door lock in a privacy mode and to correspondingly set a privacy mode as a mode for the device, wherein the electronic device updates an operation state of the security system according to the setting of the privacy mode for the door lock and the device. 21. The system of claim 20, wherein the door lock comprises at least one from the group consisting of: a virtual door lock, a room status identification device, a floor status identifier for the building, an area status identifier for building, a doorbell, and one or more sensors of a room. 22. The system of claim 20, wherein when the mode of the door lock and the device are set in the privacy mode, the receipt of notification messages to the electronic device is disabled. 23. The system of claim 20, wherein when the mode of the device is set to the privacy mode, the device is at least partially disabled from the security system, wherein the device is selected from the group consisting of: a motion sensor, a thermal sensor, a light sensor, and a video camera. 24. The system of claim 20, wherein the device refrains from providing data to the security system when the privacy mode of the device is enabled. 25. The system of claim 20, wherein the privacy mode prohibits viewing of data captured by the device of the security system via an application on the electronic device. 26. The system of claim 20, wherein the electronic device controls the enabling of a sensor of the security system for an exterior of the building and disables a sensor of the security system in an interior of the building when the electronic device sets the mode of the door lock and the device to the privacy mode. 27. The system of claim 20, wherein the electronic device disables message notifications from the device when the mode of the device is set to the privacy mode. 28. The system of claim 20, wherein when the mode of the door lock is set to the privacy mode by the electronic device, it disables at least one of the group consisting of: an audible 

 output from a doorbell for the building when a button on the doorbell is selected, and an intercom of the building. 29. The system of claim 20, wherein when the mode of the door lock is set to the privacy mode by the electronic device, the security system is controlled so as to activate a movement of window treatments corresponding to a window in a room having the door lock, so that the movement of the window treatments covers the window. 30. The system of claim 20, wherein the electronic device includes a user interface that receives the privacy mode of the security system for at least one of the group consisting of: a room of the building, and the interior of the building. 31. The system of claim 20, wherein when the electronic device sets the mode of the door lock to the privacy mode, it enables the security system to perform at least one from the group consisting of: turning off lighting in a room and reducing the light output by lighting devices within a room by a predetermined level. 32. The system of claim 20, wherein when the electronic device sets the mode of the door lock to the privacy mode, it enables the security system to close a garage door of the building. 33. The system of claim 20, wherein when the electronic device sets the mode of the door lock to the privacy mode, it disables the electronic device from receiving at least one of the group consisting of: phone calls, e-mail, and text messages. 34. The system of claim 20, wherein when the electronic device sets the mode of the door lock to the privacy mode, the electronic device is configured so as to receive communications from only a preset list of contacts. 35. The system of claim 20, wherein the privacy mode is disabled when a safety event is detected by the security system. 36. The system of claim 35, wherein the detected safety event is from a group consisting of: a detected smoke event, a detected fire event, and a break-in event. 37. The system of claim 35, wherein the security system transmits a notification to the electronic device when the privacy mode is disabled. 38. The system of claim 20, wherein the electronic device determines it is within a room associated with the door lock when the mode of the door lock is set to the privacy mode.
